_comment: Clubs & recurring groups. To add one: copy any entry, append to the list, done — the page groups by category automatically. featured: true pins it to the top.

clubs.name: Burlington Social Activities Group (BTown Brief IRL)
clubs.what: Our own Meetup — trivia nights, food crawls, kayak outings, beach hangs, and board games, plus a Saturday morning Coffee Club at Zero Gravity that draws 15-30 people. Free and open to all.
clubs.when: Several events most weeks · Coffee Club Saturdays

clubs.name: Burlington Run Club
clubs.what: A free community fun-run group that meets at Foam Brewers for a social run followed by a beer.

clubs.name: Green Mountain Athletic Association (GMAA)
clubs.what: The area's long-standing running club — group long runs, track workouts, and a full calendar of low-key local races open to everyone.
clubs.when: Sunday long runs 8am · summer track Thursdays 5:45pm

clubs.name: Long Trail Running Club
clubs.what: Trail runners of all levels sharing 4-6 mile group runs around Burlington, Winooski, Colchester, and Richmond.

clubs.name: Bolters Run Club
clubs.what: A welcoming weekly social 5K where the point is showing up and meeting people, not pace.

clubs.name: Catamount Weekly Race Series
clubs.what: The longest-running weekly race series in the country — casual trail runs and mountain bike races for all ages on Catamount's trails in Williston.

clubs.name: Green Mountain Bicycle Club
clubs.what: Vermont's oldest and largest road cycling club, with speed-grouped evening rides, weekend rides, and beginner-friendly intro rides each spring.

clubs.name: Fellowship of the Wheel
clubs.what: Chittenden County's mountain bike chapter — group events, an enduro series, and volunteer trail days on singletrack they build themselves.

clubs.name: Local Motion Community Rides
clubs.what: Burlington's walk-bike nonprofit runs casual, no-drop group rides and bike events from its Trailside Center on the waterfront.

clubs.name: Green Mountain Club — Burlington Section
clubs.what: The original 1910 chapter of Vermont's hiking club: group hikes, snowshoes, and paddles year-round, easy walks to big Long Trail days. Membership not required to join an outing.

clubs.name: Outdoors for Everyone VT
clubs.what: A Meetup built to get people of all skill levels outside enjoying Vermont, with a deliberately low bar to entry.

clubs.name: Chittenden County Chess Club
clubs.what: Chess players of all ages and abilities — casual Saturday games, plus rated Thursday-night play with the UVM Chess Club.

clubs.name: The Friendly Tabletop Gamers
clubs.what: Burlington's big board game Meetup, run by The Boardroom game café — learn-to-play sessions, mixers, and open gaming several times a week. 1,300+ members.
clubs.when: Multiple events weekly at The Boardroom (3 Mill St)

clubs.name: Quarterstaff Games
clubs.what: Vermont's oldest game store runs weekly in-store play — Friday Night Magic draws 20+ players, plus Wednesday drafts, RPGs, and casual game nights.

clubs.name: Tuesday Trivia at Vermont Comedy Club
clubs.what: A long-running weekly trivia night in the comedy club's lounge, with rotating themes and show tickets as prizes — an easy standing date for a team of friends.

clubs.name: Fletcher Free Library Book Discussion Group
clubs.what: The downtown library's monthly book discussion — register with the librarian and they'll help you get the book. They also run the food-themed 'Food for Talk' club.

clubs.name: Phoenix Books Book Groups
clubs.what: Burlington's downtown indie bookstore hosts recurring book groups — including 'Trash for the Trope,' a romance readers' group tackling a different trope each month.

clubs.name: Vermont Ultimate (Green Mountain Disc Alliance)
clubs.what: The hub for ultimate frisbee around Burlington — free summer pickup, a mixed summer league, and women/nonbinary sessions. All levels welcome.
clubs.when: Summer: pickup Thursdays · women/nonbinary Wednesdays · Saturday league

clubs.name: Game On VT
clubs.what: Chittenden County's adult rec-sports outfit — join solo or with a team for kickball, grass volleyball, floor hockey, and beginner pickleball leagues.
clubs.when: Seasonal — kickball Mondays at Leddy · volleyball Wednesdays at UVM

clubs.name: Burlington Adult Social Sports
clubs.what: A 1,900-member Meetup running seasonal coed leagues — volleyball, kickball, dodgeball, soccer, flag football — built around meeting people as much as playing.

clubs.name: Vermont Womxn's Pick-up Soccer
clubs.what: Friendly women's pickup soccer in Burlington, overseen by Parks & Rec — show up and play.

clubs.name: BVT Pickup Soccer
clubs.what: The Facebook group Burlington players use to organize casual coed pickup games around town.

clubs.name: Greater Burlington YMCA Adult Group Sports
clubs.what: Relaxed drop-in adult sports at the Y — including all-levels volleyball — no team required.

clubs.name: Pickleball South Burlington
clubs.what: The local pickleball community's home base for open play, courts, and clinics — an easy sport to pick up as a total beginner.

clubs.name: Generator Makerspace
clubs.what: Burlington's community makerspace on Sears Lane — woodshop, metal shop, laser cutters, 3D printers, jewelry and sewing studios, and a built-in community of makers.
clubs.when: Member access daily · enrollment sessions Thursdays & Saturdays

clubs.name: Knot Knite
clubs.what: A bring-your-project craft night — knitting, crochet, sewing, weaving, whatever — that gathers at Burlington breweries and bars.

clubs.name: Must Love Yarn Crafters' Groups
clubs.what: Free twice-weekly sit-and-stitch sessions at the Shelburne Road yarn shop — bring any fiber project and settle in.

clubs.name: Pause-Café Mardi (French at Fletcher Free)
clubs.what: A drop-in table where Francophones and French learners of any level meet to speak French over coffee at the downtown library.

clubs.name: Spanish Conversation Group at Fletcher Free
clubs.what: A weekly drop-in Spanish conversation circle at the library that explicitly welcomes beginners.

clubs.name: Alliance Française of the Lake Champlain Region
clubs.what: The local French cultural nonprofit — classes, monthly conversation lunches, films, potlucks, and a Tuesday conversation meetup at Burlington Bay Café.

clubs.name: South Burlington Community Chorus
clubs.what: A no-audition community chorus that welcomes anyone who likes to sing, rehearsing weekly toward a couple of concerts a year.

clubs.name: Social Band
clubs.what: Burlington's 'lively band of singers' — a friendly community chorus weaving song and poetry into seasonal concerts.

clubs.name: Irish Trad Session at Burlington Beer Company
clubs.what: A weekly open Irish traditional music session — bring your fiddle, whistle, or just a pint and listen.

clubs.name: Queen City Contras
clubs.what: Burlington's thirty-year-old contra dance series — every dance is taught, no partner or experience needed, live bands and callers at Shelburne Town Hall.

clubs.name: Vermont Swings
clubs.what: The area's Lindy Hop community — weekly beginner-friendly lessons and social dancing at North Star Community Hall, plus a monthly Saturday dance.

clubs.name: 802 Westie Collective
clubs.what: Vermont's West Coast Swing community, with recurring lessons and social dances around greater Burlington.

clubs.name: Toastmasters of Greater Burlington
clubs.what: A supportive club for practicing public speaking and leadership — guests can just show up and watch before joining.

clubs.name: Petra Cliffs Community Climbing Meetups
clubs.what: Burlington's climbing gym hosts recurring community nights — Ladies Climbing Coalition meetups and Queer Climb Nights — that make it easy to find climbing partners.
